question_text: Which of the following is an example of energy conservation in everyday life?

options.label: A
options.text: Energy is created when you burn fuel

options.label: B
options.text: Energy changes from one form to another but the total amount stays the same

options.label: C
options.text: Energy disappears when an object stops moving

options.label: D
options.text: Energy can only exist as heat

correct_answer: Energy changes from one form to another but the total amount stays the same
explanation: Energy conservation means the total energy in a closed system never changes; it only transforms between different forms like kinetic, potential, and thermal energy.
